_meta.description: Picks & shovels — the arms-dealers of the AI hardware buildout: companies that sell into the chain regardless of which end-product or brand wins. badge: hot / structural / moderate. hi = chokepoint owner highlight.

categories.title: Capital equipment — the toll booths
categories.sub: Sell to every fab and packaging house
categories.why: Whoever wins in chips, they all buy the same tools. Equipment makers monetize the entire industry's capex and own the deepest single-source chokepoints in the stack.

cards.t: Sole EUV maker; High-NA ramping. No leading-edge AI silicon exists without it. The ultimate toll booth.
US WFE trio covering most non-litho steps; KLA's metrology near-monopoly ties to yield. Recurring service base.
Japan's WFE champion, paired with ASML litho on every line. Core to every fab.
ATE duopoly with Teradyne; HBM + AI complexity lengthens test time = direct AI test-demand leverage.
The equipment chokepoint of advanced packaging; enables sub-10µm 3D stacking for HBM and chiplets.
Near-monopoly subsystem inside every deposition/etch tool — a hidden single-source pick-and-shovel.

categories.title: Materials & substrate handoff
categories.why: The unglamorous inputs that gate everything: wafers, resist, substrates, glass. Often Japanese-controlled, low-profile, and surprisingly hard to substitute.

cards.t: Japan supplies ~50%+ of wafers — upstream of even TSMC. AI ramp tightens wafer supply.
Japan controls ~80-90% of advanced resist — a deliberate strategic chokepoint demonstrated vs Korea in 2019.
High-layer-count ABF substrates for big AI dies are supply-constrained; substrate is the second packaging squeeze.
Makes the film inside every IC substrate — a hidden near-monopoly with food-company optionality.
AR waveguide glass + AI-cluster optical fiber. Materials chokepoint bordering the hard-assets map.

categories.title: Connectivity & test silicon
categories.why: As clusters scale to 100k+ GPUs, the value of moving and verifying data rises. These names sell into every accelerator and every server, agnostic to which GPU wins.

cards.t: The AI-connectivity pure-play; content scales with every GPU and rack. UALink optionality. Premium multiple.
ZeroFlap AECs dominant top-of-rack; signal integrity at 224G. High-growth connectivity niche.
The brains of transceivers + dominant switch silicon; capture the value inside China-assembled optical modules.
Every chip is tested; HBM/AI complexity lengthens test time. The duopoly monetizes complexity itself.
Power-management content per GPU rising with 1kW+ accelerators. High-quality analog compounder.

categories.title: Power & cooling — the physical wall
categories.why: AI is now gated by power and heat, not chips. These names sell the electrons and the cooling into every rack — and increasingly into the grid behind it.

cards.t: Datacenter power + thermal leader at the liquid-cooling inflection (GB200 mandates it). The cleanest power/cooling pick.
World-leading DC power supplies; 48V-to-chip transition. Quiet Taiwan AI-power compounder.
Taiwan cooling component makers riding the liquid-cooling ramp; cold plates + modules for AI servers.
Switchgear, busways, PDUs for 100kW+ racks; cross-link to grid hardening. Multi-year backlog.
Behind-the-meter gas + grid for AI power; the cross-link into the energy supply chain (see Hard Assets).

categories.title: Robotics motion components
categories.why: If humanoids scale, the value is in the actuator BOM — reducers, screws, motors — not the robot brands. These are the component chokepoints (and the China localization targets).

cards.t: Historic near-monopoly in harmonic reducers for robot joints; designed into Western humanoids. China localization is the risk.
~60% of precision RV reducers for heavy joints. The high-torque reduction-gear chokepoint.
Planetary roller screws are a humanoid linear-actuation bottleneck (with Germany's GSA). Motion-component leaders.
China's motion-control champion vertically integrating into humanoids. The clearest China-localization core.
Exclusive Tier-1 Tesla Optimus actuator assembler ($685M order). The clearest listed Optimus supply-chain proxy.
